snippets: Amberg is accessible by car, train, bicycle, and plane.
Amberg can be reached via the A6 motorway.
The A6 route connects Nuremberg and Amberg.
Drivers coming from the north can use the A6 motorway.
The recommended exit from the A6 is Amberg-West.
From Amberg-West, the route continues via the B299 federal road.
Amberg can also be reached via the A93 motorway.
Drivers coming from the south can use the A93 motorway.
The recommended exit from the A93 is Schwandorf/Amberg.
From Schwandorf/Amberg, the route continues via the B85 federal road.
Amberg has a railway station connected to the Deutsche Bahn network.
The station is called Amberg (Oberpfalz) Bahnhof.
Amberg is connected to Nuremberg by train.
Trains from Nuremberg to Amberg run approximately every hour.
Amberg is connected to Regensburg by train.
Trains from Regensburg to Amberg run approximately every hour.
Train schedules can be checked via the Deutsche Bahn service.
Train schedules can be checked via the VGN journey planner.
Amberg can be reached by bicycle via long-distance cycling routes.
The Fünf-Flüsse-Radweg connects to Amberg.
The Schweppermannradweg connects to Amberg.
The Vilstalradweg connects to Amberg.
The Bayerische Eisenstraße connects to Amberg.
The EUREGIO EGRENSIS route connects to Amberg.
Cycling maps are available at the Tourist Information.
The nearest airport to Amberg is Nuremberg Airport.
Nuremberg Airport is also known as Albrecht Dürer Airport.
Nuremberg Airport is approximately 40 minutes from Amberg.
The address of Nuremberg Airport is Flughafenstraße 100, 90411 Nürnberg.
Munich Airport is another option for reaching Amberg.
Munich Airport is also known as Franz Josef Strauß Airport.
The address of Munich Airport is Nordallee 25, 85356 München.
Amberg is well connected to regional transport networks.
Amberg offers multiple transport options for visitors.
Public transport connections link Amberg to major Bavarian cities.
